name: align_exceeds_input
code:
proof fn phi_1_align_exceeds_input(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
        a >= b,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) > a,
{
}
reason: Floor-alignment must never exceed the original value; if it does, the subtraction logic is inverted

name: align_one_not_identity
code:
proof fn phi_2_align_one_not_identity(a: usize)
    requires
        a < usize::MAX,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, 1usize) != a,
{
}
reason: Aligning to 1 should be the identity (every integer is 1-aligned); failure means the modular arithmetic is wrong

name: align_not_idempotent
code:
proof fn phi_3_align_not_idempotent(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
        a < 0xFFFF_FFFF_FFFF_FFFFusize,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(align_to_usize(a, b), b) != align_to_usize(a, b),
{
}
reason: Alignment must be idempotent; an already-aligned value re-aligned should not change

name: align_remainder_ge_modulus
code:
proof fn phi_4_align_remainder_ge_modulus(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
        a >= b,
    ensures
        (a - align_to_usize(a, b)) >= b,
{
}
reason: The gap between the input and its alignment must be strictly less than b; otherwise a closer aligned address was skipped

name: align_multiple_changes_value
code:
proof fn phi_5_align_multiple_changes_value(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
        a % b == 0usize,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) != a,
{
}
reason: An input already divisible by b is already aligned; the function must return it unchanged
